personality_one_liner: A serious, fast, and minimal self-regulation tool that feels native, precise, and unobtrusive.

spacing.rule: Generous Apple Health-style padding around content. Avoid dense dashboard-style layouts.

do's: Use on sticky headers over scrolling content
Use on bottom-sheet headers and drag handles
Ensure text on glass uses a tinted base of >=70% opacity for contrast
dont's: Do not use on generic list rows

screens.name: Landing/Login
screens.purpose: Introduce AvirLog and authenticate the user.
screens.layout: Full-bleed background image with vertical gradient scrim (transparent to surfaceInverse >=70%). Large centered app name 'AvirLog' and subtitle in the bottom 40%. 3-point explanation in clean typography. Email/password inputs and Google auth button at the bottom.

screens.name: Home/Quick Log
screens.layout: Immediate check-in format. Top header displays date. Main area dominated by three LARGE touch targets (Left, Right, Both) styled using stateLeft, stateRight, stateBoth colors. One-tap save triggers bottom sheet. Bottom sheet contains mood/energy/focus 1-10 sliders, tags wrapped in a flex-row with pill radius, and a note field.

screens.name: Today
screens.purpose: Chronological view of today's logs and quick summary.

states.empty: Subtle empty-state illustration of a blank notebook desk + 'No logs yet today. Tap Left, Right, or Both to begin.'

screens.layout: Dashboard summary at top: Total logs, percentage breakdown (% left/right/both) in minimal geometric cards. Below: vertical timeline list of logs, each showing time, state indicator, and short preview of tags/notes. Simple, generous margins.

screens.name: Insights
screens.purpose: View trends, correlations, and analysis over time.

states.empty: Abstract geometric art + 'Keep tracking. Insights will appear after 7 days of data.'

screens.layout: Apple Health-inspired. High-level charts (7-day dominance, mood/energy lines) followed by plain-language correlation cards ('Yoga tags often correlate with Left dominance'). Generous padding, clean structural dividers, glassmorphism on sticky filters.

screens.name: Calendar/History
screens.purpose: View past logs by date with edit/delete controls.
screens.layout: Calendar view at top or simple date picker. Vertical feed below showing logs for the selected date. Swipe left on a row to reveal edit/delete actions (or tap into detail view).

screens.name: Settings
screens.purpose: Manage app preferences, reminders, and account.
screens.layout: Grouped list style (Apple Settings). Profile email at top. Sections for Reminders (with interval picker), Export Data, Theme Toggle, and Delete Account. Clean icons in brandTertiary tinted squares for each row.

landing_bg.alt: Close up of soft white fabric folds, minimal texture

empty_timeline.alt: Blank notebook and pencil on desk

empty_insights.alt: Calm abstract geometric shapes in a white frame

instructions_to_main_agent: Adhere strictly to the iOS-Native Clean guidelines (Personality 1): Generous Apple Health-style padding, unobtrusive UI framing, highly readable Geist typography, and a serious, focused layout.
Use the specific state color tokens (stateLeft, stateRight, stateBoth) strictly for the three breath states. No other loud colors, blue, or purple are permitted anywhere in the app.
The Home screen should immediately present the 3 LARGE logging buttons (Left, Right, Both). Follow Stoic's UI pattern of frictionless check-ins immediately upon opening.
Ensure all interactive text overlaying the landing image has a vertical gradient scrim (transparent -> >=70% surfaceInverse opacity) behind it for WCAG contrast.
Avoid gamification, streaks, or mystical/astrological visuals. The tone is practical and precise.
Use Phosphor icons. Group settings in clean iOS-style grouped lists with icons in brandTertiary-tinted rounded squares.
